description_long: This is a hand tool with a robust, angular metal head and a solid, rectangular wooden handle. The head features two opposing jaws, one of which is fixed and the other appears to be adjustable, designed for gripping and turning nuts or bolts. The metal head is finished in a matte gray, while the jaws have a darker, almost black appearance, possibly indicating a different material or coating for added durability. The handle is a reddish-brown wood, smoothly finished and slightly tapered for ergonomic grip. The overall construction suggests durability and utility, suitable for heavy-duty tasks in urban maintenance or repair settings. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, emphasizing its utilitarian design.
description: A sturdy wrench with a matte gray metal head, dark jaws, and a smooth reddish-brown wooden handle. The tool is designed for gripping and turning fasteners, with a practical, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open jaws and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the thickness of the metal head and the flat, rectangular handle.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the jaws and the end of the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the jaw alignment and the handle's side surface.
